diagnostic_workup.purpose: Renal values, electrolytes, liver enzymes, pancreatic lipase (fPLI or Spec fPL)
Evaluate kidney function and hydration status
Screen for pulmonary masses if weight loss significant
Visualize pancreas, GI wall thickness, lymph nodes, foreign bodies or masses
If hyperthyroidism suspected after other tests

initial_management: IV fluid therapy (e.g., lactated Ringer’s) to correct mild dehydration and support renal perfusion
Anti‑emetic: maropitant 1 mg/kg IV q24h or ondansetron PO if contraindicated
Appetite stimulant: cyproheptadine 0.25 mg/kg PO q12h or mirtazapine 0.5 mg/kg PO q12h
Bland diet (prescription renal or low‑fat, high‑protein wet food) until appetite improves
Monitor weight, hydration status, and vomiting frequency twice daily
follow_up_plan: Re‑evaluate after initial lab results (within 48–72 h). Adjust fluid rate, anti‑emetic dosing, or add low‑dose steroid if IBD suspected.
If pancreatitis confirmed: consider pancreatic diet and possible short course of prednisolone.
If renal disease confirmed: initiate renal supportive therapy (dietary protein adjustment, phosphate binders).
Re‑image abdominal ultrasound 4–6 weeks after treatment initiation to assess response.
Owner education on monitoring for recurrence of vomiting or weight loss and when to seek immediate care.

notes.penicillin_allergy_consideration: Avoid penicillin-based antibiotics; use alternatives if needed.
